# KYC settings wizard
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/kyc-settings-wizard

KYC settings wizard helps you simplify and speed up the essential configuration of your KYC integration. It provides a structured checklist of key verification settings, allowing you to track which items are already completed and which still require attention. Each checklist section includes a clear description of the setting and a direct link to the relevant configuration page in the dashboard, ensuring that everything is set up correctly from the start.

Since the KYC product includes many configuration options, this solution makes onboarding easier, ensuring your account setup matches both business and regulatory needs.

# How KYC setting wizard works

The KYC settings wizard tab is located under Global Settings → User Verification in the Dashboard. It contains several configuration sections that must be reviewed and adjusted to ensure a complete and compliant onboarding setup:

- Age & Date of Birth 
- Duplicates
- PoI settings
- PoA settings
- Data Format & Processing
- Advanced Applicant Workflow settings
  - AML Screening conditions
  - High-Risk applicants
- Technical & Security

Each section contains a specific configuration description. Every setting includes a checkbox, allowing you to track whether it has already been completed. 

> 📘 Note
> 
> The checkbox state applies to your entire account and can be updated by members with the Manage Company Settings permission.

You can also click a setting to view more details, such as:

- Short explanation of the setting.
- Link to relevant documentation.
- Direct link to the configuration page in the dashboard.

Where possible, the wizard links you directly to the exact setting at the level and highlights it. In other cases, the link takes you to the closest relevant screen.

================================================================================

# Email and phone verification
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/email-and-phone-verification

You can check the applicant's phone number and email address to determine its reliability.

# How email and phone verification works

The email and phone verification procedure works as follows:

1. Applicants provide their email address and/or phone number and get a one-time code (or OTP).
2. Applicants are requested to confirm the code (or OTP), and if it is correct, the step is passed.
3. The system checks the data and, according to the internal logic, returns the results as red, yellow, or green labels, depending on how much risk the specified email address or phone number poses.

> 📘 Note
> 
> For more information on how we calculate email and phone risk level, see this article.

# Get started with email and phone verification

To start using email and phone checks, do any of the following:

- Submit the applicant data via this API method.
- Collect the applicant data via the WebSDK or MobileSDK.

> 👍 Tip
> 
> - Combine standard email and phone verification with digital footprint checks to get a detailed picture about your applicants.
> - You can test email and phone verification in Sandbox mode. Once a daily texting limit expires, use the `111111` code to test SMS sending for phone verification.

# Review email and phone verification results

To view the verification results:

1. In the Dashboard, go to the Applicants page and select a profile.
2. Navigate to the Checks section and review the summary of the extracted and verified data: 
   1. Phone confirmation check
   2. Email confirmation check

The result of email or phone number verification is displayed as an OTP Verified label. It indicates the credibility and authenticity of the email or phone number, verified by sending a one-time password (OTP) code.

> 📘 Note
> 
> If applicants do not provide the correct code, the system prevents them from proceeding with the verification process.
> 
> There are only two possible outcomes:
> 
> - Correct code provided — verification continues, and the corresponding check block is marked as `GREEN`.
> - Incorrect code provided — verification cannot be completed, and no check result block is displayed. The system requests applicants to resubmit the verification code.

================================================================================

# Fastfail
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/fastfail

Fastfail is a pre-verification step that quickly evaluates whether a submitted document is suitable for further verification, allowing the system to provide immediate feedback and, in rare cases, avoid unnecessary processing.

Instead of running full verification immediately, the system performs a set of automatic checks in advance to filter out clearly unsuitable inputs. This helps your applicants quickly identify issues and fix them before continuing with the full verification flow.

When a document is uploaded to Sumsub, the system runs a set of initial checks to detect the following:

- All pages and sides of the document are uploaded.
- The uploaded images represent a valid document (not a non-document image).
- The same side of the document is not submitted twice.
- Document number, name fields, and MRZ are present, clearly visible, and can be recognized.
- Document image is not a screenshot or a printout.
- Image has not been cropped.
- Document is not considered expired or issued in the forbidden territory in accordance with the settings.
- Photo in the document contains the human face.
- Image metadata does not indicate the use of prohibited editing software.

If the Fastfail check is not passed, the system notifies the applicant and requests a new document upload.

> 🚧 Important
> 
> Fastfail does not replace full verification and is designed to detect only obvious issues. It does not perform detailed quality checks, which are handled at later stages of the verification process. Passing Fastfail does not guarantee final approval.

# Error rejection tags

Errors do not let applicants proceed with verification using the presented document.

| Tag                         | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| :-------------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `forbiddenDocument`         | Unsupported or unacceptable type/country of the document.                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| `missingImportantInfo`      | Some of the required document data is missing or cannot be read.                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| `dataNotReadable`           | Data on the image is missing or cannot be read.                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| `expiredDoc`                | Document is expired.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| `documentWayTooMuchOutside` | Some parts of the document are cropped.                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| `noIdDocFacePhoto`          | Face is not clearly visible on the document.                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| `screenshot`                | Provided image is a screenshot.                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| `sameSides`                 | Image of the same side of the document was uploaded as front and back sides.                                                                                                                                                                            |
| `shouldBeMrzDocument`       | Provided document type should have an MRZ, but there is no readable MRZ on the image.                                                                                                                                                                   |
| `shouldBeDoubleSided`       | Sides of the document should be provided.                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| `shouldBeDoublePaged`       | Full double-page photo of the document (usually, two main passport pages) is required.                                                                                                                                                                  |
| `documentDeclinedBefore`    | Same image was uploaded earlier by the same applicant and declined by the system.                                                                                                                                                                       |
| `mrzNotReadable`            | We could not extract the MRZ code from the uploaded document. This may happen due to a variety of reasons, such as poor image quality or some corners of the document missing, which may include the MRZ area.                                          |
| `docExpiresSoon`            | The submitted document is about to expire in N months. This is defined by the Minimum residual validity of ID document setting in the Dashboard. |
| `missingDob`                | The document does not contain the owner's date of birth.                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| `incompleteDob`             | The document does not contain the full date of birth (day, month, and year).                                                                                                                                                                            |

# Warning rejection tags

Warnings let applicants proceed with verification after retaking the document photo and providing missing information.

| Tag                      | Description                                               |
| :----------------------- | :-------------------------------------------------------- |
| `badSelfie`              | Face or the photo in the document is not clearly visible. |
| `dataReadability`        | Information in the document cannot be read.               |
| `inconsistentDocument`   | Uploaded photos do not belong to the same document.       |
| `maybeExpiredDoc`        | Document may be expired.                                  |
| `documentTooMuchOutside` | Document is not fully in the frame.                       |

================================================================================

# Periodic verification
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/periodic-verification

Periodic verification offers the ability to automatically conduct ongoing due diligence for KYC and KYB applicant checks, which minimizes compliance risks and streamlines daily operations. Periodic verification conditions are based on 3 main parameters:

- Applicant data
- Timeframes
- Actions applied to the selected applicants

This ensures organizations can stay compliant while reducing manual effort and scaling their operations with confidence.

# How periodic verification works

You can set rules based on any applicant’s parameter available at Sumsub like registration date, residency, verification status, geography, risk level, and more. 

- For instance, for KYC, regulations may require applicants to periodically resubmit PoI or PoA documents, or complete a Liveness check after X days of registration or starting for the review date, after a specific action completion,  once a document expires and many more.
- For KYB customers, automated periodic rechecks help reduce compliance risks by confirming that the business address is still valid, company information is up to date, and UBOs are not on sanctions lists.
- For the Travel Rule, periodic verification helps enforce deadlines by triggering actions after a certain time period has passed. For instance, automatically rejecting or returning funds if no response is received within the required timeframe.

Easily manage and configure periodic verification via rules: one rule represents a single scheduler logic, giving you a simple way to control all your recheck scenarios.

> 📘 Note
> 
> Once configured, the scheduler runs automatically every day at 00:00 CET, applying recheck logic only to approved applicants.

# Set up periodic verification

The following is a sequence of steps to be taken to set up the conditions for the periodic verification.

> 📘 Note
> 
> You do not need to enable Travel Rule or Transaction Monitoring to use periodic verification, as it is a separate product. These features are configured from the same tab but are not related to periodic verification.

## Step 1: Create custom rule

To create a rule for the periodic verification, do the following:

1. In the Dashboard, navigate to the Transactions and travel rule section.
2. Go to the Rules page and click Create rule.
3. Give your rule a preferred name and optional description.
4. In the Rule behavior, select the Trigger scheduled event option.
5. In the Affect applicant section, define what action should be applied to the applicants who met the criteria of the scheduler.

## Step 2: Configure scheduler conditions

First, define the criteria for which applicants should be included in the periodic check.

- Select Time since a level was completed, if you want the system to re-evaluate all applicants who passed a specific level after a certain period of time. To do this, you will also need to specify the number of days after the applicant completed the level and level itself.
- Select A custom condition, if you want to filter applicants by custom logic. In the Timer condition field, you can specify those parameters (applicant country, source key, applicant tag and so on).

If needed, you can optionally apply the scheduler only for applicants created from the specific date by checking the Activation scope box.

In the Affect applicant section, add actions as described here. Additionally to the default settings, you are able to reset steps for a current or new level. To do so, check the Reset box next to the step of your interest.  
If you want to add more actions, click Add action.

## Step 3: Test configuration

You can test a rule during editing, without saving it, by clicking the Test button. The system will then display sample applicants who meet the condition.

## Step 4: Save changes

After completing the setup, click Create rule in the top-right corner.

> 📘 Note
> 
> If you go live, the event will not trigger right away, but at 00:00 CET of the next day.

## Step 5: View results

You can see scheduled events on the Transactions table in the Dashboard by applying the  Type filter and selecting the Scheduled event. They will appear for each applicant who meets the conditions.

================================================================================

# Ongoing document monitoring
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/ongoing-document-monitoring

Ongoing document monitoring is a process where, once a day, the system looks for applicants that have an active identity document containing a suitable validity date. 

The validity date may be less than or equal to the present day or the present day minus the number of days specified in the settings. Such applicants are assigned the >  status and their documents are rechecked for validity.

If the documents are expired, applicants receive the > status, and once the new documents are submitted, applicants will be verified again and get the corresponding verification status.

> 👍 Tip
> 
> Learn more about the verification statuses in this article.

# Configure ongoing document monitoring

Configure ongoing document monitoring to make sure your applicants submit active documents:

1. In the Dashboard, open the Ongoing Document Monitoring page.
2. Choose the document validity option that suits your particular case.
3. Save your changes.

> 📘 Note
> 
> The change takes effect within 24 hours for applicants created before the setting was enabled.

================================================================================

# Age Estimation
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/age-estimation

The Sumsub’s Age Estimation solution uses the in-house Liveness technology to determine an individual's age based on their facial features and also can detect sophisticated fraud attempts with video footage, masks, and deepfakes. 

One of the main cases for the technology is to detect teenage users who need parental consent or cannot use applications at all.

The algorithms usually allow for a non-significant 1-2 year difference. You can use these estimations to tailor the verification flow as needed.

To identify underage users accurately, we suggest using an age buffer of 3, 7, or 10 years, depending on the sector. For example, implementing a 10-year buffer would mean that any user who appears to be 28 years old or younger will be asked to present an ID. If your case allows for fewer restrictions, we propose using a 7-year buffer, which means having an age threshold of 25 years.

This strategy can be easily implemented through our condition-based Workflow builder.

> 😀 Did you know?
> 
> Sumsub is approved by the German Commission for the Protection of Minors in the Media (KJM) as an age verification solution in line with German regulatory standards.

# Get started with Age Estimation

Use age estimation to route applicants to different verification flows, depending on their age and the probability level established by the system:

1. Contact us to enable the age estimation feature.
2. Set up the WebSDK and MobileSDK integration.
3. Add Advanced Liveness or a Web camera photo check to the level step.
4. Conduct verification and review results.

================================================================================

# Cross Check Rules
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/cross-check-rules

Cross checks compare applicant data from multiple sources (for example, user-provided fields, extracted document data, outputs of other checks, and external services). Cross Check Rules let you define which fields are compared and how they are compared during the verification process.

Use cross check rules to:

- Detect inconsistencies between sources (for instance, applicant input and document data).
- Reduce fraud risk by flagging mismatched identity attributes.
- Standardize decisioning by applying the same comparison logic across multiple verification levels.

Cross Check Rules are typically used to validate consistency between identity attributes across sources, such as comparing an applicant’s name and date of birth input against document extraction results. When third-party data is available, you can also use them to compare address and other profile fields against external sources. Due to this functionality, cross checks are an essential part of the Sumsub AllDocs Verification used for processing a wide range of applicant documents.

Depending on your configuration, these checks can help flag meaningful mismatches for review while allowing expected variations (for example, transliteration or wrong spelling) where appropriate.

# How Cross Check Rules work

Cross Check Rules operate through presets of rules that you apply to one or more verification levels. In the preset list, the Levels column shows where a preset is currently applied. 

Each rule has:

- Comparison function.
- Arguments that include the paths to the data you want to compare, and value parameters that set comparison mode.

For instance, you want to compare the applicant address extracted from their documents and the address provided by the applicant. In such a case, the comparison function will be `addressesCompatibleOrNull`, which applies the address comparison logic, and arguments will include the following:

- `applicant.info.address` — Address information extracted from the applicant documents.
- `applicant.fixedInfo.address` — Address information provided by the user via SDK/API.

To compare the applicant name extracted from their documents and the name provided by the applicant, use the namesCompatibleOrNull function.

Cross Check Rules engine also supports the `dobCompatibleOrNull` function for the date of birth comparison.

You can also set the comparison mode by defining the level of fuzziness, which represents the allowed degree of variation in spelling.

If the specified conditions are not met, we will reject the applicant. The system will show the rejection reason that you specified when configuring the preset.

# Get started with Cross Check Rules

In the Dashboard, go to the Global settings in the Integrations section and navigate to Data Comparison.

You will see the list of presets with the following details:

- Preset name.
- Verification levels the given preset applies to.
- Columns with timestamps when it was created and modified. These columns display the exact dates and time, as well as information about who made the changes.

In the top menu, you can also delete a preset or refresh the layout by selecting the columns you want to be shown on the page.

Each preset can operate in the following modes:

- Basic mode represents the general data comparison settings.
- Advanced mode allows you to configure a preset.
  > 📘 Note
  > 
  > To customize a preset in advanced mode, contact your Custom Success Manager or our support team.

To configure data comparison settings in basic mode:

1. Configure Address comparison mode: 
   - Fuzzy — allows spelling variations in the name of the country, region, street, and so on: Churchill-laan 266/III and Churchill-Yaan 266/III are acceptable.
   - Strict — allows only an exact match.
2. Configure Name comparison mode (compares the name entered by the applicant manually with the name in their documents): 
   - Weak containment — a flexible name matching that converts names to lowercase and removes spaces for broader matches: John Manuel Smith will match with Johnmanuel Smith.
   - Moderate — allows a wide range of spelling: John M Smith and Dr. J Manuel Smith are acceptable
   - Strict — allows only an exact match: Dr. John Smith and John Smith are acceptable, John D Smith and John Smith are not.

When configuring Name comparison mode, you can also select to:

- Ignore middle name mismatches. Switches off middle name comparison.
- Ignore provided personal info. Switches off comparison of the proof of address document data against the provided personal information.

## Create custom preset

Perform the following steps to create your own preset.

### Step 1: Set up preset

To set up a new preset, complete the following actions:

1. In the Dashboard, go to the Global settings in the Integrations section and navigate to Data Comparison.
2. Click Create preset.
3. [Optional] Select function and fill in the Arguments fields.
4. [Optional] Click Add to apply more conditions, or provide an alternative rules group by clicking Or group.
5. In the Otherwise reject step block, specify which step will be rejected if the conditions are not met and the rejection reason that we will display to the applicant.
6. If needed, you can duplicate the existing preset by clicking Duplicate in the preset menu.

### Step 2: Apply preset to levels

After you created the preset, apply it to the level of your interest:

1. Open the Cross Check Rules tab.
2. In the preset list, check the Levels column and click a level name to open it.
3. Inside the level editor, select the preset and click Save.

> 📘 Note
> 
> You can use the same preset on multiple levels. Changes to the preset apply to all levels this preset is used on.

# Applicant profile overview
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/applicant-profile-overview

The Overview tab of the applicant page is a complex navigation and statistics section displaying checks performance data and highlighting the products involved in verification.  

The tab is divided into a few parts—the Insights block and Applicant dashboard section, which contains various widgets. 

The insights block includes: 

- General information about an applicant — status, age, onboarding date, country of residence, email and phone number, along with their respective confirmation statuses.
- User verification details — AML screening data, applicant level, and the number of duplicate profiles.
- Risk overview section — Transactions score and associated risk labels. 

The Applicant dashboard section displays product-specific widgets that present data on completed checks and related events from the past three months. This section is customizable and may include different widgets depending on the purchased solutions:

- User Verification widgets — Workflow runs, Last actions, User verification events, and Payment methods.
- Transaction Monitoring widgets — Transactions, Transactions turnover, Transactions count by status, and Transactions timeline.

> 📘 Note
> 
> - Clicking on specific checks within the Overview tab will take you to their respective detail pages, where you can view more information about performance and results.
> - If a product has not been purchased, a widget mockup will be shown along with information about that solution and its features.

# Set up Overview tab

You can add, delete, and change placement of the widgets within the section. 

1. Go to the Overview  tab of any applicant profile and navigate to Applicant dashboard.
2. Click Add widget and select the desired elements in the pop-up window.
3. To remove or rearrange widgets, click Edit.
4. Use the drag handle icon to move elements and click the bin icon to delete them.

> 🚧 Important
> 
> All the widgets settings are saved for a particular user account and are not specific to applicant profiles—you and each member of your team can adjust the Overview tab depending on the needs.

        "https://files.readme.io/0e41935f9d25320013d95bb3af824bc2784215227560bd35dedcf987f0dbeff9-webhooks-rounded-border-visible.png",
        "",
        ""
      ],
      "align": "center"
    }
  ]
}
[/block]

# Notes

Notes help you preserve and share information (or memos) that you or Sumsub operators may have saved to emphasize important aspects related to the given applicant. For example, when we decline an applicant as a possible PEP, and you decide to approve this applicant, you can let us know about your decision by leaving a note. 

Tags and file attachments can also be added to a note to provide context and supporting information.

[block:image]
{
  "images": [
    {
      "image": [
        "https://files.readme.io/5562a5bc94446699e3f0657f03243db4ab2257f4bae2db36c9d570d047956092-notes-grey-frame-rounded-padded.png",
        "",
        ""
      ],
      "align": "center"
    }
  ]
}
[/block]

# Fraud segmentation notes

Fraud segmentation notes are system-generated records stored in the Notes section to provide an additional layer of information regarding rejection reasons. Each note includes the following information:

- Identification number of modified image (video)
- Fraud type (category)
- Affected security features
- Alteration characteristics

[block:image]
{
  "images": [
    {
      "image": [
        "https://files.readme.io/cd3a47e9668e858608c566090a88601af2dc10af273d3da1435dfdac0123c5b2-screenshotroundedcorners.png",
        "",
        ""
      ],
      "align": "center"
    }
  ]
}
[/block]

> 📘 Note
> 
> - Fraud segmentation notes are visible only to Dashboard users with the Admin role.
> - You can edit and delete fraud segmentation notes, but you cannot assign them to applicant profiles manually.

Below you can find detailed information on each fraud category, outlining the associated security features, applicable document types, and specific signs of alteration.

- Print Layer
- Material
- Optical
- Electronic
- Additional
- Edited data
- Deepfake
- Proof of address
- Template

## Print Layer

Print layer fraud refers to various techniques used to imitate advanced printing-based security features.

[block:html]

## Material

Material fraud involves the falsification or replacement of the document’s base material and integrated security components, compromising structural and physical authenticity.

## Optical

Optical fraud targets the visual or light-responsive security elements of documents.

## Electronic

Electronic fraud involves various manipulations aimed at falsifying digital and machine-readable security features.

## Additional

Additional fraud refers to mixed manipulations targeting supplementary security mechanisms.

## Edited data

The Edited data category identifies the specific personal data points to which alterations have been applied.

## Deepfake

Deepfake refers to AI-generated or synthetically manipulated facial imagery or video used to falsify identity.

## Proof of address

The Proof of address category identifies the specific PoA document data points to which alterations have been applied.

## Templates

Template refers to a document template associated with elevated risk indicators.

# Risk labels

Risk labels are assigned during verification and indicate certain characteristics of an applicant. For more information, see this article.

        ""
      ],
      "align": "center"
    }

# Applicant information

The Applicant information section contains the applicant’s personal details, provided information, and data extracted from uploaded documents.

Additional documents can also be uploaded here if needed.

## Profile information

The Profile information shows top-level applicant details, such as applicant email address, phone number, language, and source key. You can change or update this information at any time.

[block:image]
{
  "images": [

        "",
        ""
      ],
      "align": "center"
    }
  ]
}
[/block]

## Add Documents and Fields

In this section, you can manually upload additional documents or add other document fields with information needed during the applicant’s verification process. For example, when performing a manual check or running Video identification. Documents that are not used for verification can also be added to enrich the applicant’s profile. For more information on how to add documents, refer to this article .

[block:image]
{
  "images": [

      "image": [

      "align": "center"
    }
  ]
}

## Personal info

These sections show the following:

- Extracted information — displays the information recognized and extracted from the applicant documents. It is updated automatically when document data is changed.
- Provided information — displays the information submitted by the applicant via the WebSDK or MobileSDK or via this API method.
- Address — displays address information collected during verification:
  - Extracted from the applicant's PoA documents.
  - Submitted by the applicant via the WebSDK and MobileSDK.
    > You can also submit this information using this API method.

# Documents

Documents displays the applicant’s identity documents and the information automatically extracted from them. If the verification includes a questionnaire, the submitted answers are shown here as well.

Here, you can also:

- Add documents to the applicant by selecting its type and country and uploading the file.
- View all document files with its data in one tab by clicking See photos.  

[block:image]
{

      ],
      "align": "center"
    }
  ]
}
[/block]

## Questionnaire

The Questionnaire section shows a questionnaire required to be completed by the applicant during or after verification. An operator who runs a video identification can also complete the questionnaire using the answers provided by the applicant.

After the questionnaire is complete, you can find the submitted answers and uploaded files in the Questionnaire section.

> To resubmit the information for a completed questionnaire, you can reset the questionnaire by clicking Reset. Alternatively, you can use this API method providing the `QUESTIONNAIRE` parameter.

# Checks

The Checks section includes the results of all checks required for verification, such as identity data and proof of address checks, email and phone confirmation, and other checks depending on the current verification level.

## Identity Data Verification

Identity Data Verification indicates check results showing the reliability of the provided PoI documents:

- The Provided data section shows the data obtained from the trusted government and commercial sources by the applicant PoI document number.
- The Retrieved data section indicates the check status of the submitted document.

Based on the extracted data, we can confirm the validity of the submitted document:

- The green () check label means that the identity document is valid.
- The red () check label means that the document is invalid or it was not possible to extract the required data.

> The timestamps that you can find next to the name of the check indicate the date and time when the given check was performed. They are also highlighted in the color corresponding to the check result.

Depending on the check results, the system assigns a label explaining the given status as per the following table.

## Proof of address check

Proof of address checks indicate check results showing the reliability of the provided PoA documents.

The results may include the following parameters:

1. Layout and format	
   - Misaligned margins
   - Misaligned tables
   - Inconsistent spacing
   - Unusual borders
   - Incomplete or broken table rendering
   - Different fonts in one document
   - Different font sizes in one document
2. Image quality	
   - Mixed sharpness when some parts of the image are clear, and some are blurred
   - Cropped or pasted areas with visible edges or color differences
   - Pixel noise around text
3. Data Inconsistencies 
   - Lack of consistency of dates throughout the document
   - Inconsistent language and localization
   - Unrealistic billing amounts

The following table explains the check labels, their description, and possible check states as indicated by colors:

- The green () check label means that the identity document is valid.
- The yellow () check label means that it was not possible to extract enough data for a given check.
- The red () check label means that the address information is invalid.

                       Acceptable type\n                      \n                      \n                          Shows whether the type or subtype of PoA document is acceptable.\n — IDCARD).\n                      \n                  \n                  \n                      \n                          Address check\n                      \n                      \n                          Shows whether the business address or PO box is acceptable.\n                      \n                      \n                          Red () — Business address or PO box is forbidden by client settings.\n                      \n                  \n                  \n                      \n                          POI and POA are different documents\n                      \n                      \n                          Verifies that PoI and PoA documents do not match.\n                      \n                      \n                          Red () — PoI and PoA are the same document, which is forbidden by client settings.\n                      \n                  \n                  \n                      \n                          Matches Proof of Identity country\n                      \n                      \n                          Indicates if PoI country matches with PoA country.\n                      \n                      \n                          Red () — PoI and PoA countries do not match.\n                          Gray () — Сheck is disabled in client settings.\n                      \n                  \n                  \n                      \n                          Acceptable region\n                      \n                      \n                          Verifies that the applicant address does not belong to disputed territories.\n                      \n                      \n                          Red () — PoA document contains the address of disputed territories.\n                      \n                  \n                  \n                      \n                          Acceptable language\n                      \n                      \n                          Reveals if PoA document contains languages forbidden by your settings.\n                      \n                      \n                          Red () — PoA contains languages forbidden by client settings.\n                      \n                  \n              \n          \n      \n  \n"
}
[/block]

## AML screening

During the auto-check, Sumsub uses the watchlist screening to determine if your applicants belong to any of the various sanction lists and watchlists across the globe.

The watchlist screening helps find applicants that are:

- Known or suspected terrorists
- Sanctioned persons
- Politically exposed persons (PEPs)
- Persons with criminal background
- Persons mentioned in adverse media

All applicants that belong to any of these categories are rejected automatically during the watchlists (WL) screening. To approve such applicants, follow the instructions given in this article.

To see everything that happens to an applicant’s AML screening check, you can view the AML screening events log.

> You can also generate a screening report and save it as PDF.

## Liveness check

Liveness shows the results of the Liveness & Face match check passed by the applicant.

You can see such statistics as the date and time when the Liveness check was performed, information about the platform and device used during the Liveness session, view a video snippet, and pictures from the recorded selfie.

> 📘 Note
> 
> The Liveness check is performed only if the Selfie step (Advanced Liveness check) is added to the verification level.

## Face match

Face match displays the verification results that indicate whether the person in the identity document (PoI) is the same person presenting the selfie. The system does this by comparing facial representations extracted from both images.

Face match works in the following way:

1. Face localization. The system identifies the portrait on the identity document using a database of known document templates (for example, passports, ID cards, driver’s licenses). This allows it to determine the face location for each document type and use the correct image region for further processing.
2. Face detection and alignment. The document image and the selfie are processed to detect the face and align it into a consistent format. This step accounts for differences in orientation, scale, and lighting conditions.
3. Feature extraction. Each detected face is converted into a numerical representation (embedding or feature vector). These vectors capture patterns learned from large datasets and encode the identity of a person for comparison.
4. Face comparison. The embeddings from the document image and the selfie are compared to evaluate whether they correspond to the same identity. This comparison is based on overall similarity rather than any single feature.
5. Face match result. The system determines whether the two images correspond to the same person. The result is a match or no-match outcome, displayed on the applicant page and used in the identity verification process.

[block:image]
{
  "images": [
    {
      "image": [
        "https://files.readme.io/a7412b8cb9e92695dd9af40356607533a57083567145676bb6542564bddb758d-facematchrounded.png",
        "",
        ""
      ],
      "align": "center"
    }
  ]
}
[/block]

## Duplicates

Shows duplicate profiles.

To detect duplicates, Sumsub searches for profiles that have the same full name, ID documents, email addresses, phone numbers, or even facial features.

For more information on how to configure duplicate processing, see this article.

# Photos

The Photos section contains a list of documents submitted by applicants during verification in accordance with the verification level settings.

For example, you may want to request applicants to submit identity documents, such as a passport, driver's license, or resident permit, along with some additional proof of identity, such as the second identity document (for example, student ID or vehicle passport) and/or residence permit, and so on.

Each of the documents submitted by your applicants is listed independently, one after another, showing its verification status and rejection reasons (if any).

You can perform the following actions on documents:

- Change the current status  of any document if needed.
- Edit document information in case the OCR engine could not recognize it properly.
- Change document information, such as its country and type, and remove the document.

## Attachments

In the Attachments** section, the applicant can upload specific documents that were not included in the standard set of documents for certain checks. You can use such documents on other stages. 

For example, the applicant needs to complete the questionnaire with a particular document, such as Form W8-BEN, and provide a Water Bill. It can be marked as a PoA document, but we do not request it during the regular PoA check.

# Transaction check results
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/transaction-check-results

On the Transactions tab, you can find the details of applicant transactions.

# Overview

The Overview section contains:

- Applicant notes that can help you highlight significant information about the applicant transactions. You can also add tags to notes to organize and categorize them.
- Assessment that shows the score indicating potential risks. If the risk level changes, it will be reflected here. For more information on how to set up ongoing risk assessment, see this article.
- Applicant Transactions that contains the applicant transaction table. This table is updated in accordance with the actions of the applicant.

# Events

When the transaction status is updated, a new transaction event is automatically created, capturing any changes. The Events section contains a table with such events for future reference.

> 👍 Tip
> 
> For further use of the table, you can download it as a CSV file.

# Analytics

Analytics provides valuable insights into the applicant's financial behavior and transactions. This information can help in assessing the risks, detecting any fraudulent activity, and making more informed decisions. The data is presented here as reports and graphs. To learn more about what kind of data is displayed in analytics, see this article.

To view the information for the period you are interested in, you can set a filter and select a time period. The data can also be downloaded as a CSV file.

# Muted rules

This section contains a list of rule alerts that have been muted for a certain period. You can mute specific rules when you need to prevent risk score accumulation.

# Network

In the Network section, you can see the data on who the applicant is conducting transactions with. It can reveal any suspicious or potentially fraudulent activities. 

The connections between the applicants (if any) are also displayed here. It can help detect frequent transfers between accounts or multiple accounts being used by the same individual or group.

================================================================================

# Applicant action check results
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/applicant-action-check-results

On the Actions tab, you can find the applicant action check results.

# Action events

Each action may have a sequence of events associated with it. For example, when applicants upload selfies or when operators change the status of a specific step within the action and other similar updates, all such actions are logged in as events for future reference.

Once the event is logged, the following information becomes available:

- The event that occurred.
- The activity that has created the event and the date and time (GMT) when the event occurred.
- The platform type and location (IP address and optionally the country and city) where the event occurred (if available).

================================================================================

# Manage applicant profiles
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/manage-profiles

With Sumsub verification, you have a wide range of actions available when managing profiles of your applicants. Сreate new profiles, approve or reject applicants manually, and adjust their verification status as needed. You can also manage documents within a specific applicant profile — add new files or make inactive those you do not want considered during verification. Additionally, you can assign tags to applicants for better organization, change the verification level for a profile, request a profile recheck at any point, and take many other actions to help you manage applicants your way.

# Create and access applicant profiles

# Manage applicant profile statuses

# Review and verify applicant profiles

# Optimize and organize applicant profiles

================================================================================

# Create applicant profile
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/create-applicant-profile

An applicant is a person or legal entity to be verified.

Applicants are created automatically when they pass verification in the WebSDK or MobileSDK. 

To see the list of applicants, go to the Applicants page in the Dashboard and choose between Individuals and Companies.

# Create applicant profile

To create a new applicant profile:

1. Go to the Applicants page and, depending on whether you want to create a profile for a physical person or company, select Individuals or Companies, respectively.
2. Click New applicant or New company.
3. Select a verification level for the applicant to pass.
4. Click Generate WebSDK applicant link to generate a permalink and send it to the applicant, or select Create applicant manually and provide information about your applicant. The lifetime of a permalink is 30 minutes.

> Alternatively, you can use this API method or verification links to create an applicant profile.

> 📘 Note
> 
> The maximum number of applicant profiles that you can create in Sandbox mode is limited to 500 per 24 hours.

# Configure applicant settings

To configure general applicant settings:

1. Go to the Applicant settings tab.
2. Provide an email address of your technical support department that your applicants can use in case of rejection.
3. Set the file size that can be uploaded to the WebSDK or on the applicant page in the Dashboard.
4. Choose the order in which applicant names should appear in the profile and lists.
5. Select a source from which applicant names should be taken.
6. Save your changes.

# Export applicant list

You can export the list of applicants as a CSV file. Such a file contains extended information on each of your applicants, such as, for example, the creation date, last review date, reject type, reject level, and so on.

To export the list, click CSV export in the upper-right corner of the Individuals and Companies pages.

> 📘 Note
> 
> - CSV export is available only if your Dashboard account has been granted appropriate privileges.
> - A short delay is possible during the export session, especially when you have large applicant lists; make sure to wait until the CSV file is fully compiled and downloaded. The export is guaranteed if the number of applicant records in the list does not exceed 1000. If you experience trouble downloading the file, try to limit the number of applicant records by applying filters.
> - You cannot sort certain columns for a CSV file prior to exporting. If selective data is required, contact our support team.

================================================================================

# Approve and decline applicants manually
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/approve-and-decline-applicants-manually

You can approve and decline applicants manually in case the verification decision was made incorrectly.

# Manage applicant status

To approve or reject applicants:

1. In the Dashboard, go to the Applicants page and open an applicant profile.
2. In the upper-right corner, click Change applicant status. Upon initiating the applicant status change, the profile goes into the Requires action status.
3. Recheck the profile:  
   - For documents and selfies, click Change status and set the resubmission requested and rejected labels as necessary.
   - For watchlists, change the match status to False positive.
   - For Liveness & Face match and Summary, manually edit the check results as necessary by clicking the pencil icon.
4. Select Approve or Reject, depending on your verdict.

When you select Reject, you will be prompted to choose the rejection reason. Once you set the rejection reason, click Reject applicant.

> You will have the option to customize the message that will be shown to the applicant in case of rejection. To do so, open the permission settings and make sure that the Use moderation buttons checkbox is not selected.

> 📘 Note
> 
> - You can approve or reject an applicant regardless of their status, except when there are documents in the Pending status. In this case, an error message will be returned. The Documents requested status requires all requested documents to be uploaded before the status can be changed. For more information about applicant statuses, see this article.
> - Members with the Admin role can edit rejection comments (Resubmission requested or Rejected) directly in the applicant profile when changing the status for documents, selfie, or the entire profile. This means they can adjust both labels and any rejection comment that will be displayed to the end user in the SDK.
> - The Approve button is not available in the following cases:
>   - An approval request is in progress.
>   - There are red flags in Face match, cross-validation, or watchlist checks.
>   - There are unresolved watchlist hits that need to be handled before approval.
>   - There are reject reasons that need to be removed to approve the applicant.
>   - There are rejected documents that need to be handled to approve the applicant.
> - The Reject button is not available in the following cases:
>   - An approval request is in progress.
>   - There are unresolved watchlist hits that need to be handled before approval.
>   - Not enough permissions.

# Reset SPAM rejection

Change the verification status of an applicant profile that has been rejected for SPAM:

1. In the Dashboard, open an applicant profile and in the top-right corner, click Change applicant status. 
2. Click Reject and unselect the SPAM label and other rejected labels (all red labels) if you want to let the applicant continue verification. 
3. Select the resubmission requested labels (blue labels), scroll down, and click Request resubmission, so the applicant will be able to resubmit their documents or complete a verification step again.

> 📘 Note
> 
> The Sumsub support team cannot handle this process due to security requirements and for the purpose of logging actions in the system.

================================================================================

# Approve rejected applicants
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/approve-rejected-applicants

We automatically reject applicants in cases the system fails to recognize any data in the uploaded documents or identifies a mismatch in the provided information. 

If you want to approve such rejected applicants nevertheless, you can do it manually. Manual approval enables you to change the flow of the verification and the final decision even after the automated checks have failed.

To approve rejected applicants manually, you have to resolve the reason for rejection while reviewing the applicant.

> 🚧 Attention
> 
> Some errors cannot be resolved manually. If any of these errors occur, you should use Force approve.

# Issues that can be resolved manually

In case the errors can be resolved manually, the process of approving rejected applicants includes the following steps:

1. In the Dashboard, go to the Applicants page.
2. Open the applicant profile and make the necessary changes depending on the error.
3. Click Save.
4. Go to the Review panel and click Approve.

> When you click Approve, the system initiates the remaining checks. If the remaining checks are successful, the applicant will be approved. In case of other errors, repeat steps 2-4.

## Face match

This error occurs when the system fails to match a digital facial profile with a stored image of an applicant.

To resolve this:

1. In the applicant profile, go to the Summary block.
2. Click on the pen icon on the Face Match line and change the result to Accept.
3. In the Review panel, click Approve.

## OCR error

This error occurs if the OCR algorithms fail to recognize any data in the uploaded documents.

To resolve this:

1. In the applicant profile, go to the Document information block.
2. Correct the data and click Save.
3. Accept the document.
4. In the Review panel, click Approve.

## Similar search

During this check, the system searches for the applicant's duplicates. If any have been found, the error occurs.

To resolve this:

1. In the applicant profile, go to the Summary block.
2. Click on the pen icon on the Similar search line and change the result to Accept.
3. In the Review panel, click Approve.

## Cross-check

Cross-checks are based on matching different applicant data. If the system indicates data inconsistency or account duplicates, an error will occur. It may happen in different situations, for example, when the applicant’s name on the PoI and PoA documents does not match.

To resolve this:

1. In the applicant profile, go to the Summary block.
2. Click on the pen icon on the Cross check line and change the result to Accept.
3. In the Review panel, click Approve.

## Watchlists

Watchlists contain lists of known or suspected terrorists, money launderers, fraudsters, sanctioned persons, or PEPs. If the applicant matches any of the watchlist records, the error will occur.

To resolve this:

1. In the applicant profile, scroll down to the Watchlist block.
2. Review each hit.
3. In the Review panel, click Approve.

> 📘 Note
> 
> If at least one hit with True positive/Potential match/Unknown result exists, the applicant cannot be approved. To approve such case, every hit with a state different from False positive should be marked as Whitelisted.

## AUTO_REGULATIONS

This error occurs when the system detects the wrong country and/or date of birth based on the applicant's information.

To resolve this in case of the wrong date of birth:

1. In the applicant profile, go to the Document information block.
2. Correct date of birth and click Save.
3. In the Review panel, click Approve.

To resolve this in case of the wrong country:

1. In the applicant profile, go to the Document block.
2. Correct the country of the document and click Save.
3. In the Review panel, click Approve.

# Issues that cannot be resolved manually

The following checks cannot be resolved manually as they are the results of third-party checks:

- Email check
- IP check
- Phone check
- Background check

To approve such checks:

1. In the Dashboard, go to the Applicants page.
2. Open the applicant profile.
3. In the Review panel, click on the right side of the Approve button.
4. Choose Force approve.

> In case of using Force approve, the system will ignore these checks and change the status of the applicant to Approved, bypassing any pending checks.

> ❗️ Important
> 
> When using Force approve, the approval is your responsibility. Sumsub is not responsible for this decision or its consequences.

================================================================================

# Request profile check
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/request-profile-check

You can request a new check of an applicant profile if you want us to perform applicant verification.

For example, you can request a check when you believe a mistake was made by the system during the previous check or you are sending the applicant documents via the API and would like us to check the profile.

# Request check

To request a check:

1. In the Dashboard, go to the Applicants page and open an applicant profile.
2. In the top menu, select Request check. 

Upon requesting the check, the profile receives a Pending status and is sent to us for verification.

> Alternatively, you can use this API method to request the profile check.

> 📘 Note
> 
> The Request check button is not available for profiles that are in Pending, Queued, Prechecked, or Rejected statuses.

================================================================================

# Reset applicant profile
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/reset-applicant-profile

Resetting the applicant profile verification state allows you to mark all the documents uploaded by the applicant as inactive and delete all verification statuses assigned earlier. 

You may need to reset the current profile state if you want the applicant to repeat verification from the beginning and re-upload their documents.

# Reset profile

To reset the profile:

1. In the Dashboard, go to the Applicants page and open an applicant profile.
2. In the top menu, select Reset applicant.

> Alternatively, you can use this API method to reset the profile.

> 📘 Note
> 
> - It is not recommended to reset a rejected applicant. Such cases may involve restricted countries or potentially fraudulent applicants.
> - You cannot reset profiles with Pending, Processing, or Prechecked statuses.
> - After resetting the profile, the data obtained from the previous attempts will not be taken into account.

================================================================================

# Make applicant profile inactive
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/make-applicant-profile-inactive

The Inactive status is a strategic tool that enhances the fairness of the verification process while maintaining the integrity of the fraud detection system. It provides flexibility, allowing you to manage applicants without disrupting essential security protocols. This status supports a balanced, efficient approach to applicant management that prioritizes both fairness and system integrity.

When an applicant profile is marked inactive, it will be marked along with its data, such as the uploaded documents, photos, and videos as inactive. The system automatically disregards inactive profiles, and your applicants will have to resubmit their personal details from scratch.

Sumsub may mark an applicant profile as inactive in the following cases:

- When a client confirmed testing.
- When an applicant needs to be unblocked to make a new verification attempt in a system.

You can also mark a profile as inactive if you want the applicant to recreate it without producing duplicates.

# Make profile inactive

To make a profile inactive:

1. In the Dashboard, go to the Applicants page and open an applicant profile.
2. In the top menu, select Mark as inactive.

> 📘 Note
> 
> Marking an applicant as inactive does not constitute a final onboarding or rejection decision. To reactivate the applicant, click the Mark as active button on the applicant page. This action allows you to proceed with approving, rejecting, or requesting additional documentation as needed.
> 
> For further alignment with your internal procedures, a webhook can be enabled to track and notify of any changes to an applicant inactive status.

================================================================================

# Make applicant documents inactive
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/make-applicant-documents-inactive

The option of marking documents as inactive provides you with the ability to remove irrelevant documents from the verification process without the need for permanent deletion.

You can make any type of document inactive. Mind that the system does not allow to deactivate documents of the applicants currently being checked with the `pending`, `queued`, or `prechecked` status.

After you deactivate the document, its photo and the data extracted during the check remain in the system. The step at which the document was uploaded gets the `Requested` status. In this case, the status of the applicant does not change. 

If you deactivate the document and then run the SDK, the applicant status will be changed to `Documents Requested`. 

> 📘 Note
> 
> In case our system declines the document during the check, it will be uploaded to the applicant profile and automatically marked as inactive. We will ask the applicant to provide new photos.

# Make document inactive

To deactivate the applicant document, follow these steps:

1. In the Dashboard, go to the Applicants page.
2. Select the Individuals or Companies section and open the profile of interest.
3. Scroll to the Photos block and find the desired document.
4. Click Mark as inactive.

To revert this action, complete the same steps and click Revert inactive.

================================================================================

# Blocklist applicants
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/blocklist-applicants

Blocklisting applicants lets you prevent fraudsters and forgers from repeatedly accessing your products and services.

Use the Internal blocklist, which is maintained by your operators and officers, to restrict access for particular applicants. When blocklisting applicants, they are automatically added to the autogenerated My Default Blocklist list.

> 📘 Note
> 
> To blocklist applicants, your account must be granted the Blocklist applicants permission.

# Add applicants to blocklist

To add applicants to the blocklist:

1. In the Dashboard, go to the Applicants page and open an applicant profile.
2. In the top menu, select Change applicant status and click Reject.
3. Specify the rejection reasons: go to Final rejection and select Blocklist. 
4. Click Reject applicant. 

> Alternatively, you can use this API method to blocklist applicants.

# Handle identity documents from blocklisted applicants

In certain cases, blocklisted applicants may try to pass verification again by uploading documents via multiple accounts. To manage such applicants effectively and make reasonable decisions, mind the following:

- Documents rejected by forgery. If a document has been rejected with the Forgery tag—it is considered high-risk and approval of the applicant is not recommended.
- Approved documents. If a document has been approved, it indicates that the document has passed verification, which confirms its authenticity and validity. If there are no issues associated with the applicant on your side, you may consider approving the profile.

# Remove applicants from blocklist

To exclude applicants from the blocklist: 

1. Go to Client lists in the Dashboard. 
2. Open the My Default Blocklist list.
3. Paste the applicant ID into the search bar to locate the required profile, then click the trash icon to remove it.

================================================================================

# Add applicant tags
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/add-applicant-tags

Applicant tags are custom labels that you can create and then add to applicant profiles for better filtering and classification.

# Create and assign tags

To create a tag:

1. In the Dashboard, go to the Applicant tags tab and select Create tag at the bottom of the tag list.
2. Provide a name for the new tag and select a tag color. 
3. Select the Include tag to applicant summary report checkbox if you want to see tags in your reports.

Once the tag is created, you can assign it to any of your applicants:

1. In the Dashboard, go to the Applicants page and open an applicant profile.
2. Click Add tag at the top of the profile page and select tags to add.

> Alternatively, you can use this API method to add tags.

================================================================================

# Manage verification level
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/change-verification-level

You can easily adjust the initial verification level if you want to reset specific steps or apply a different verification level with new step configurations.

# Manage verification level

To make changes to the verification level:

1. In the Dashboard, go to the Applicants page and open an applicant profile.
2. Navigate to the Verification tab and, under Summary, click Manage level.
3. To configure resubmission:
   1. Select the Reset checkbox next to the required verification steps.
   2. Click Update current level.
4. To switch the verification level:
   1. From the Level drop-down list, select a new verification level.
   2. Click Change level.

Once the changes are made, you can send the verification link by email or copy it to share manually. Enter the applicant’s email address and select Send link, or simply copy the link.

> 🚧 Important
> 
> - Levels cannot be changed when the applicant is being verified.
> - You can only reset steps that are included in the current level.

================================================================================

# View summary report
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/view-summary-report

An applicant summary report contains personal applicant information, such as the applicant name, profile data, statuses of all checks, uploaded documents, and detailed description of check results.

# View report

To generate a report:

1. In the Dashboard, go to the Applicants page and open an applicant profile.
2. At the top of the profile page, select Summary report.
3. From the appearing menu, you can select which sections you want to include in the applicant summary report. 

> 📘 Note
> 
> Our system will remember your preferences, so you will not have to select those sections every time you generate a report.

================================================================================

# Send email with verification status
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/send-email-with-verification-status

Applicants can be notified automatically by email every time their verification status changes. This happens if the email notifications are enabled in the verification level settings.

You can also manually send updates about the current verification status.

# Email verification status to applicant

To send an email with the verification status:

1. In the Dashboard, go to the Applicants page.
2. Select the Individuals or Companies section and open the profile of interest.
3. From the menu in the top-right corner, select Email status to applicant.

Once confirmed, an email message with the current verification status will be sent to the email address specified in the Profile data section of the applicant profile.

> 📘 Note
> 
> - The Email status to applicant option will not be available if the applicant email address was not provided.
> - If resubmission is required, the email would also include a Verify me link which is available for 7 days.

================================================================================

# Add notes
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/add-notes

The Notes section is an optional feature in applicant profiles that can be used to add details that may be important for your team or Sumsub officers to consider. When performing a manual review of an applicant profile, notes help teams better understand unusual decisions and see the full context of the case.

The most common use case for Notes is requesting approval of an applicant profile or document regardless of a decision previously made by the system. For example, if you want to accept a document that was rejected as unsuitable, you can provide additional explanation using Notes.

> ❗️ Important
> 
> Do not use Notes to upload documents for verification. Documents relevant to the verification must be uploaded to the Documents section.

# Attach notes 

To add notes:

1. Go to the Dashboard and navigate to Applicants. 
2. Open the required applicant profile and go to the Verification tab. 
3. Locate the Note section and enter necessary details. 
4. Add files and tags if needed.
5. Click Add note.

================================================================================

# Get applicant data with Custom CSV Export
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/custom-csv-export

Custom CSV Export allows you to get large volumes of data about applicants who pass identity verification.

# How Custom CSV Export works

The process of generation of a custom CSV export includes the following steps: 

1. A Sumsub client opens the Individual applicants page and ensures that they have access to the Custom CSV Export feature.
2. If there is appropriate permission granted to the profile, they click the Custom CSV Export button and select the profiles and data to export.
3. Once done, Sumsub starts the report generation and redirects the client to the report page.
4. The client reviews the report details and status.
5. The client receives a notification of the readiness and downloads the report.

# Benefits

The Custom CSV Export brings the following advantages:

- Ability to export a broad range of applicant information.
- Option to choose which data to export.
- Flexible permission system that allows you to restrict sensitive data exports.
- Support for exporting large volumes of data.
- Export history is maintained for up to 30 days.

# Use cases

Exporting applicant data might be helpful within many business processes, such as:

- Performing analytics and statistics, gaining a more in-depth understanding of applicants, and segmenting data.
- Creating reports and calculations for marketing purposes.
- Preparing data and reports for auditors and regulators.
- Importing data into your system to automate internal processes.
- Conducting additional quality control checks on your side.

# Applicant Data available for Custom CSV export

All data is divided into two groups — sensitive and non-sensitive. This separation allows us to manage access to particular information using different permissions.

# Technical limitations of Custom CSV Export feature

Custom CSV Export has some specific technical characteristics regarding the data retention period and filters that should be considered while using the feature.

## Mismatch between filtered and exported entries

The number of exported applicant profiles may differ from the number of filtered entries in the Dashboard. This discrepancy is usually caused by the following reasons:

- Applying a specific period of time. The Period filter operates differently in the Dashboard and Custom CSV Export. In the Dashboard, your local time zone is generally used, while the export feature uses the UTC time zone (UTC+0). Therefore, the applicant lists may vary.
- Using unsupported filters. Some filters are not supported in Custom CSV Export, so applicants intended to be removed might be mistakenly included in the export.

## Data retention period

All reports are stored for 30 days and are available for download during this time. The availability period of a report can be found on its page. After this period, reports are permanently deleted, and access to them is completely lost.

## Search unavailability

You cannot use the search bar to find applicant profiles while using the Custom CSV Export feature. Instead, use the applicant filters located under the search bar.

## Limitations of filters

Before you create a custom CSV export, you sort applicants via filters that are available on the Applicants page.  
The feature supports almost all filters listed on the page. However, there are minor inconsistencies in how the filters operate when you use them for creating a CSV report.

# Enable Custom CSV Export notifications

Each account in the Sumsub Dashboard has its notification settings, which determine the information the account can receive. 

You can enable the Custom CSV Export notifications by doing the following:

1. Open the Dashboard and click the Profile icon in the upper-right corner.
2. Select Profile settings.
3. Navigate to the Notifications tab.
4. Find Custom CSV export notifications and set up how you want to receive the alerts.

The Custom CSV Export notifications are sent when the report is ready and contain a link to the report page.

# Configure Custom CSV Export permissions

To allow your team to create custom CSV exports via the Dashboard, you should grant them access using the following permissions:

- Custom CSV export  (Lower sensitive data) (downloadAnonymousApplicantReport)  
  Allows to export data that is not classified as sensitive.
- Custom CSV export (Higher sensitive data) (downloadSensitiveApplicantReport)  
  Allows to export of all types of data, including sensitive ones.

These are the steps to be taken to manage the roles and give access to the feature:

1. In the Dashboard, go to Settings and select Team.
2. Navigate to Roles.
3. Select a role or create a new one.
4. Choose one of the permissions listed above. If you do not have these permissions in the list, contact the Owner of your key, so they can set it up for you.
5. Besides the mentioned permissions, include two permissions that give access to the list of applicants and their personal data: 
   - See list of applicants
   - See applicant’s personal data

> 📘 Note
> 
> We recommend granting permission to export sensitive data only to a very limited group of individuals.

## Who has access to Custom CSV reports

Access to the report is granted exclusively to the account that requested it. The report will not be accessible via a direct link, even if the users are on the same key and possess the full set of necessary permissions.

## Restrict creating Custom CSV exports

There are additional settings that can be applied to the Dashboard profiles to impose limits on using the Custom CSV Export feature:

- The See only sandbox environment data access limitation. The access limitations are set in Roles.
- The See only those applicants that are created by this account, or via a link that this account generated account limitation.
- Available source key values based on the specified restrictions in Source Key Restrictions.
- The Hide approved applicants for this account access limitation.

# Export applicant data

To create a custom CSV export:

1. In the Dashboard, navigate to the list of applicants and find the Custom CSV Export button in the upper-right corner of the page. If you can see the button, it means that you have been granted appropriate permissions.
2. Sort applicants using the filters on the page. Note that some filters may work differently when you create a CSV report.
3. When filtered, select which profiles to export:
   1. To export specific profiles from the filtered list, check the box to the left of the applicant’s name.
   2. To export all profiles, no action is required.
4. Click the Custom CSV Export button to Initiate the process of creating the report. You will be asked to select the data to be exported. The set of fields can be modified and the system will remember selection on your device.
5. (Only applicable to users with 2FA enabled) Complete two-factor authentication by entering the code.
6. Review information on the report and its status. Once you have selected the data for export, Sumsub starts the report generation and redirects you to the page where you can find all the details regarding your report:
   1. Report Name. The name of your report.
   2. Current Status. The status of your report. The possible statuses are: Requested, Completed, Failed.
   3. Availability Period. Information on how many days the report will remain available for download.
   4. Download Button. This button is available once your report is ready.
   5. Report Information. Information on the author (user who requests a report), generation date, report type, file size, and number of entries. 
   6. Filters Used. The filters that have been applied to generate the report.
   7. Report Columns. The columns that are included in the report.
   8. Delete report Button. The button for deleting reports. 
7. When you receive a notification of the readiness, download the report—follow the link and download an archive containing the report (CSV) and the separate file with its settings.

# View available reports

You can find all the reports you have access to (including those generated by you within the last 30 days) on the dedicated Reports page. On this page, you can view and download reports from the list.

================================================================================

# Applicant statuses
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/applicant-statuses

During each verification, an applicant receives a status that conveys the current verification state.

To see the current verification state of an applicant profile, in the Dashboard, go to the Applicants page and in the Status column, review the status.

> You can also apply the Review status -> Check completed filter to see profiles with the following statuses: Approved, Resubmission requested, and Rejected.

}
[/block]

# Enable notifications about applicant status change

The following is a sequence of steps to be taken to enable notifications about applicants being moved to the Requires action status:

1. Go to the Profile settings and open Notifications.
2. In the table, find Applicant requires action.
3. Enable the Send by Email and/or Display on Dashboard toggles.

================================================================================

# Applicant risk labels
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/applicant-risk-labels

Risk labels are indicators assigned during the verification process to highlight certain characteristics or potential risks associated with applicants. 

By default, risk labels do not influence verification flows and not taken into account when our system is passing decision on specific applicant. However, it is possible to setup automated workflows that will trigger specific actions—like manual review or rejection—for an applicant based on the risk labels assigned to them. For example, you can use risk labels to detect VPN access and reject applicants for using a VPN connection. 

# View risk labels

To see the risk labels:

1. In the Dashboard, go to the Applicants page.
2. Open an applicant profile and scroll down to the Risk labels section.
